title: Production of GTPase Deficient RAB1A(Q70L) Protein

steps: Transform E. coli BL21 cells with the pET vector containing 6x-His-RAB1A(Q70L) by heat shock.
Plate the transformed cells on ampicillin-resistant plates.
Incubate the plates Overnight at 37 °C .
Inoculate a single colony into 10 mL of LB broth.
Incubate the culture Overnight at 37 °C .
Transfer the entire pre-culture into a 1 L culture flask.
Grow the 1 L culture at 37 °C with shaking at 220 rpm, °C, until the optical density (OD) reaches approximately 0.6.
Place the flask in an ice bath for 00:15:00 to cool rapidly.
Induce protein expression by adding 0.1 millimolar (mM) IPTG.
Continue incubating the culture Overnight (approximately 16 hours) at 18 °C with shaking at 180 rpm, °C, .
Harvest the cells by centrifugation at 4000 rpm, , in a BECKMAN Coulter centrifuge.
Wash the cell pellet once with PBS.
Pellet the cells again at 4000 x g, , .
Flash freeze the pellet in liquid nitrogen.
Store the frozen pellet at -80 °C until purification.
Thaw the frozen cell pellet to Room temperature .
Resuspend the cells in lysis buffer. Lysis buffer %s %s HEPES pH 7.5 25 mM NaCl 300 mM MgCl2 2 mM TCEP 1 mM
Add an EDTA-free protease inhibitor tablet (Thermo Fisher) and PMSF to a concentration of 1 millimolar (mM) .
Lyse the cells by sonication using a 5-second on, 5-second off cycle at 50% power for a cumulative time of 00:06:00 .
Centrifuge the lysate at 17000 rpm, , 45 minutes to clarify.
Apply the supernatant to a Ni-NTA column (3x).
Wash the column until the wash buffer is free of protein, as measured by a Bradford assay.
Elute the protein using a wash buffer containing 300 millimolar (mM) imidazole.
Concentrate the eluted protein.
Apply the concentrated protein to an S75 10/300 column in buffer containing 25 millimolar (mM) HEPES pH 7.5 , 150 millimolar (mM) NaCl, 1 millimolar (mM) MgCl 2 , and 1 millimolar (mM) TCEP.
Concentrate the purified protein to a final concentration of 50 micromolar (µM) .
Divide the protein into 50 µL aliquots.
Flash freeze the protein aliquots in liquid nitrogen.
Store the frozen protein aliquots at -80 °C for future use.
creator: Annan SI Cook
